    95→

    97→interface StoredData {
    98→  workspace: Workspace

   102→
   103→export class SessionStore {
   104→  private configPath: string
   105→  private backupPath: string
   106→  private data: StoredData
   107→
   108→  constructor() {
   109→    const configDir = join(app.getPath('userData'), 'config')
   110→    if (!existsSync(configDir)) {
   111→      mkdirSync(configDir, { recursive: true })
   112→    }
   113→    this.configPath = join(configDir, 'workspace.json')
   114→    this.backupPath = join(configDir, 'workspace.json.backup')
   115→    this.data = this.load()
   116→  }
   117→
   118→  private loadFile(path: string): StoredData | null {
   119→    try {
   120→      if (!existsSync(path)) return null
   121→      const stat = statSync(path)
   122→      if (stat.size === 0) return null
   123→      const content = readFileSync(path, 'utf-8')
   124→      const data = JSON.parse(content)

   114→function handlePtyStreamUpgrade(
   115→  req: any,
   116→  socket: any,
   117→  head: any,
   118→  ptyId: string,
   119→  deps: WebSocketManagerDeps
   120→): void {
   121→  const ptyManager = deps.getPtyManager()
   122→  if (!ptyManager || !ptyManager.getProcess(ptyId)) {
   123→    log('PTY stream upgrade failed - PTY not found', { ptyId })
   124→    socket.write('HTTP/1.1 404 Not Found\r\n\r\n')
   125→    socket.destroy()
   126→    return
   127→  }
   128→
   129→  const streamWss = new WebSocketServer({ noServer: true })
   130→
   131→  streamWss.handleUpgrade(req, socket, head, (ws) => {
   132→    log('PTY stream connected', { ptyId })
   133→
   134→    const ptyStreams = deps.getPtyStreams()
   135→    if (!ptyStreams.has(ptyId)) {
   136→      ptyStreams.set(ptyId, new Set())
   137→    }
   138→    ptyStreams.get(ptyId)!.add(ws)
   139→
   140→    ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'connected', ptyId }))
   141→
   142→    // Flush any buffered data
   143→    flushPtyBuffer(ptyId, ws, deps.getPtyDataBuffer())
   144→
   145→    ws.on('message', (message: Buffer) => {
   146→      try {
   147→        const msg = JSON.parse(message.toString())
   148→
   149→        switch (msg.type) {
   150→          case 'input':
   151→            if (msg.data && ptyManager) {
   152→              ptyManager.write(ptyId, msg.data)
   153→            }
   154→            break
   155→
   156→          case 'resize':
   157→            if (msg.cols && msg.rows && ptyManager) {
   158→              ptyManager.resize(ptyId, msg.cols, msg.rows)
   159→            }
   160→            break
   161→
   162→          case 'ping':
   163→            ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'pong', timestamp: Date.now() }))
   164→            break
   165→
   166→          default:
   167→            log('Unknown PTY stream message type', { type: msg.type, ptyId })
   168→        }
   169→      } catch (e) {
   170→        log('Invalid PTY stream message', { error: String(e), ptyId })
   171→      }
   172→    })
   173→
   174→    ws.on('close', () => {
   175→      log('PTY stream disconnected', { ptyId })
   176→      ptyStreams.get(ptyId)?.delete(ws)
   177→      if (ptyStreams.get(ptyId)?.size === 0) {
   178→        ptyStreams.delete(ptyId)
   179→      }
   180→    })
   181→
   182→    ws.on('error', (err) => {
   183→      log('PTY stream error', { error: String(err), ptyId })
   184→      ptyStreams.get(ptyId)?.delete(ws)
   185→    })
   186→  })
   187→}
   188→
   189→function handleWebSocketMessage(ws: WebSocket, msg: any, deps: WebSocketManagerDeps): void {
   190→  const ptyManager = deps.getPtyManager()
   191→  const terminalSubscriptions = deps.getTerminalSubscriptions()
   192→
   193→  switch (msg.type) {
   194→    case 'subscribe':
   195→      if (msg.ptyId) {
   196→        if (!terminalSubscriptions.has(msg.ptyId)) {
   197→          terminalSubscriptions.set(msg.ptyId, new Set())
   198→        }
   199→        terminalSubscriptions.get(msg.ptyId)!.add(ws)
   200→        ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'subscribed', ptyId: msg.ptyId }))
   201→      }
   202→      break
   203→
   204→    case 'unsubscribe':
   205→      if (msg.ptyId) {
   206→        terminalSubscriptions.get(msg.ptyId)?.delete(ws)
   207→      }
   208→      break
   209→
   210→    case 'write':
   211→      if (msg.ptyId && msg.data && ptyManager) {
   212→        ptyManager.write(msg.ptyId, msg.data)
   213→      }
   214→      break
   215→
   216→    case 'resize':
   217→      if (msg.ptyId && msg.cols && msg.rows && ptyManager) {
   218→        ptyManager.resize(msg.ptyId, msg.cols, msg.rows)
   219→      }
   220→      break
   221→
   222→    case 'ping':
   223→      ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'pong', timestamp: Date.now() }))
   224→      break
   225→  }
   226→}
   227→
   228→function flushPtyBuffer(ptyId: string, ws: WebSocket, ptyDataBuffer: Map<string, string[]>): void {
   229→  const buffer = ptyDataBuffer.get(ptyId)
   230→  if (!buffer || buffer.length === 0) return
   231→
   232→  buffer.forEach(data => {
   233→    if (ws.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN) {
   234→      ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'data', data }))
   235→    }
   236→  })
   237→
   238→  ptyDataBuffer.delete(ptyId)
   239→}
   240→
   241→function sendPendingFilesToClient(ws: WebSocket, pendingFiles: Map<string, PendingFile>): void {
   242→  const now = Date.now()
   243→  // Clean up expired files first
   244→  for (const [fileId, file] of pendingFiles) {
   245→    if (file.expiresAt < now) {
   246→      pendingFiles.delete(fileId)
   247→    }
   248→  }
   249→
   250→  const files = Array.from(pendingFiles.values()).map(f => ({
   251→    id: f.id,
   252→    name: f.name,
   253→    size: f.size,
   254→    mimeType: f.mimeType,
   255→    message: f.message
   256→  }))
   257→
   258→  if (files.length > 0) {
   259→    ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'files:pending', files }))
   260→  }
   261→}
   262→
   263→export function broadcastTerminalData(
   264→  ptyId: string,
   265→  data: string,
   266→  terminalSubscriptions: Map<string, Set<WebSocket>>
   267→): void {
   268→  const subscribers = terminalSubscriptions.get(ptyId)
   269→  if (!subscribers) return
   270→
   271→  const message = JSON.stringify({
   272→    type: 'terminal-data',
   273→    ptyId,
   274→    data
   275→  })
   276→
   277→  subscribers.forEach(ws => {
   278→    if (ws.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN) {
   279→      ws.send(message)
   280→    }
   281→  })
   282→}
   283→
   284→export function broadcastPtyData(
   285→  ptyId: string,
   286→  data: string,
   287→  ptyStreams: Map<string, Set<WebSocket>>,
   288→  ptyDataBuffer: Map<string, string[]>
   289→): void {
   290→  const streams = ptyStreams.get(ptyId)
   291→
   292→  // If no streams connected, buffer the data
   293→  if (!streams || streams.size === 0) {
   294→    if (!ptyDataBuffer.has(ptyId)) {
   295→      ptyDataBuffer.set(ptyId, [])
   296→    }
   297→    const buffer = ptyDataBuffer.get(ptyId)!
   298→    buffer.push(data)
   299→    // Limit buffer size
   300→    if (buffer.length > 1000) {
   301→      buffer.shift()
   302→    }
   303→    return
   304→  }
   305→
   306→  const message = JSON.stringify({
   307→    type: 'data',
   308→    data
   309→  })
   310→
   311→  streams.forEach(ws => {
   312→    if (ws.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN) {
   313→      ws.send(message)
   314→    }
   315→  })
   316→}
   317→
   318→export function broadcastPtyExit(
   319→  ptyId: string,
   320→  code: number,
   321→  ptyStreams: Map<string, Set<WebSocket>>,
   322→  terminalSubscriptions?: Map<string, Set<WebSocket>>
   323→): void {
   324→  const streams = ptyStreams.get(ptyId)
   325→  if (streams && streams.size > 0) {
   326→    const message = JSON.stringify({
   327→      type: 'exit',
   328→      code
   329→    })
   330→
   331→    streams.forEach(ws => {
   332→      if (ws.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN) {
   333→        ws.send(message)
   334→        ws.close(1000, 'PTY exited')
   335→      }
   336→    })
   337→  }
   338→
   339→  // Clean up stale subscriptions for this PTY
   340→  ptyStreams.delete(ptyId)
   341→  terminalSubscriptions?.delete(ptyId)
   342→}

content.text:
I see there's a sandboxing issue with bash. Let me proceed with my analysis based on the code I've already read. The files themselves don't show obvious changes, so let me analyze them for the issues you asked me to check:

## Code Review Report

### 1. **src/main/session-store.ts** — Save/Load/Backup Logic

**(a) Does the fix look correct?** YES, generally sound.

**(b) Bugs introduced?** None critical, but one edge case:

- **Race condition potential (minor)**: Lines 174-187 back up the existing file BEFORE writing the temp file. If a crash occurs between the backup write (line 181) and the atomic rename (line 197), recovery works, but there's a brief window where both old and new data are on disk. This is acceptable for most use cases, but in high-concurrency scenarios, consider backing up AFTER successful temp file verification.

**(c) Edge cases missed?**

- **Line 195 verification is good** but doesn't validate the backup file was successfully written. If backup write fails (line 181 catches it), the error is logged but processing continues. This is actually correct — the backup failure shouldn't block the main save.
- **Missing temp file cleanup on crash**: If the process crashes between writeFileSync (line 191) and renameSync (line 197), the `.tmp` file persists. Not dangerous (it's ignored), but consider adding a cleanup routine on initialization to remove stale `.tmp` files.
- **Zero-size file handling (line 122)**: Good, but doesn't account for truncated JSON (partial writes). The validation at line 195 mitigates this.

**Rating: SAFE** ✓

---

### 2. **src/main/mobile-server/routes/workspace.ts** — POST Handler Field Unwrapping

**(a) Does the fix look correct?** **NO — CRITICAL BUG FOUND**

Look at lines 67-89 (POST /api/workspace):
```javascript
const incoming = req.body
const incomingProjects = incoming?.workspace?.projects?.length || 0  // Line 75
```

But line 84 passes the full req.body to saveWorkspace:
```javascript
sessionStore.saveWorkspace(req.body)  // Line 84
```

Compare to the PUT handler (lines 43-65):
```javascript
const incoming = req.body
const incomingProjects = incoming?.projects?.length || 0  // Line 51
sessionStore.saveWorkspace(req.body)  // Line 60 - same object
```

**The inconsistency**: POST unwraps `req.body.workspace.projects`, but PUT expects `req.body.projects` (without the nested `.workspace`).

**What saveWorkspace expects** (line 214 of session-store.ts):
```javascript
saveWorkspace(workspace: Workspace)
```

So the caller should pass `{ projects, openTabs, ... }`, NOT `{ workspace: { projects, ... } }`.

**(b) Bug introduced?** **YES**

- POST sends `{ workspace: { projects, ... } }` to saveWorkspace
- saveWorkspace does `this.data.workspace = workspace` (line 222)
- This creates `this.data.workspace = { workspace: { projects } }` — **nested wrapper!**

GET returns the correct structure (line 37 returns `workspace` directly).

**(c) Edge cases missed?**

- If a client calls POST with the structure that matches PUT (just `{ projects, ... }`), the line 75 check fails: `incoming?.workspace?.projects` is undefined, so it treats it as 0 projects, potentially blocking a valid save.

**Rating: CRITICAL** ✗

**Fix**: Change line 84 to `sessionStore.saveWorkspace(req.body.workspace || req.body)` or simply `sessionStore.saveWorkspace(req.body)` and fix the validation logic on line 75 to match PUT.

---

### 3. **src/main/mobile-server/middleware.ts** — CORS Hostname Validation

**(a) Does the fix look correct?** **MOSTLY YES, but one bypass concern.**

**(b) Bugs introduced?** **POTENTIAL BYPASS**

The hostname validation allows:
- `localhost`, `127.0.0.1` (line 44)
- RFC 1918 private IPs (line 50)
- Tailscale CGNAT and MagicDNS (lines 55-64)

**Issue**: The origin parsing at line 37 uses `new URL(origin).hostname`, which correctly extracts the hostname. However:

- **Whitespace in origin**: If someone crafts `origin = "https://localhost "` (trailing space), `new URL()` may throw and be caught at line 38, correctly blocking it.
- **Port injection**: `new URL("http://attacker.com:localhost")` — the `localhost` becomes the port, not hostname. `new URL().hostname` correctly returns `attacker.com`. Safe.
- **Missing `://` scheme check**: Line 30 checks for `capacitor://` and `file://` schemes before parsing, which is good. But what if origin is `capacitor://attacker.com` or `file:///attacker.com`? These pass the prefix check. For mobile apps, this is likely intentional (capacitor is trusted), but document it.

**(c) Edge cases missed?**

- **Case sensitivity**: Hostname comparison (line 44, 62) is case-sensitive. Domain `LOCALHOST` would not match. However, hostnames are technically case-insensitive in HTTP, and browsers normalize them. This is likely a non-issue but could be paranoid-safe: `hostname.toLowerCase()`.
- **Regex 1918 bounds check**: Line 50's regex `/^(10\.\d+\.\d+\.\d+|...)/` correctly validates RFC 1918. But `/\d+/` matches 0-999, not just 0-255. Example: `192.168.256.1` would pass the regex but is invalid. However, this is fine since it's checked at connection-time against the actual resolved IP, not arbitrary input.
- **Tailscale CGNAT edge case** (line 56): `/^100\.(6[4-9]|[7-9]\d|1[01]\d|12[0-7])\.\d+\.\d+$/` — this is correct. The second octet range 100.64.x.x to 100.127.x.x is correct for CGNAT.

**Rating: SAFE** ✓ (with caveat: capacitor:// and file:// are trusted by design)

---

### 4. **src/renderer/components/beads/adapters/kspec-adapter.ts** — Module State & Reconnect Logic

**(a) Does the fix look correct?** **MOSTLY YES, but threading concern.**

**(b) Bugs introduced?** **RACE CONDITION IN STATE**

- **Lines 28-30** use module-level flags `_daemonEnsuredFlag` and `_daemonEnsuredForCwd`.
- **Multiple instances problem**: If two `KspecAdapter` instances exist (e.g., two projects open), they share the same global `_daemonEnsuredFlag`. 

Example race:
1. Instance A calls `ensureDaemon("/project/a")`
2. Instance B calls `ensureDaemon("/project/b")`  
3. Instance A's health check fails, sets `_daemonEnsuredFlag = false`
4. Instance B is now incorrectly in a "daemon not ensured" state

**Impact**: Moderate — the reconnect logic (line 135) will retry, so it's self-healing, but causes unnecessary API calls.

**(c) Edge cases missed?**

- **CWD switch logic** (lines 96-98): When switching from `/project/a` to `/project/b`, the flag resets. But if a fetch error occurs in the middle of processing `/project/b`, the flag is set to false. Then returning to `/project/a` still thinks the daemon isn't ensured. Consider: `_daemonEnsuredForCwd` is set correctly, but `_daemonEnsuredFlag` is global.

- **Reconnect timer cleanup** (lines 278-280): The `unwatch()` method clears the timer. Good. But if `connectWs()` (line 246) throws an exception before setting `this.ws`, the catch block is empty, so no timer is scheduled. Next `watch()` call will retry. This is acceptable.

- **WebSocket message filtering** (lines 252-255): Only processes `tasks:updates` and `task_updated` events. What if the daemon sends a `ping` or other event? It's ignored, which is fine for this adapter.

**Status mapping** (lines 53-66):
- `in_progress` → `in_progress` ✓
- `completed`, `cancelled` → `closed` ✓
- `pending`, `blocked`, `pending_review`, `needs_work` → `open` ✓
- Correct for a task tracker.

**Rating: MODERATE** ⚠ — The global state should be instance-specific. Store in `this.` instead of module scope.

---

### 5. **src/main/orchestrator-api.ts** — Port Retry Logic

**(a) Does the fix look correct?** **YES**

**(b) Bugs introduced?** None detected.

Lines 56-78 implement exponential port trying:
- Starts at `ORCHESTRATOR_PORT` (19836)
- Retries up to `MAX_PORT_RETRIES` (5) times
- Each retry increments by 1
- The recursion via `this.tryListen(port + 1)` is safe because it's bounded by `MAX_PORT_RETRIES`

The check at line 57 correctly computes retry count as `port - ORCHESTRATOR_PORT`.

**(c) Edge cases missed?**

- **Error listener cleanup** (line 70): Each call to `tryListen()` adds a new `.once('error')` listener. If `tryListen(19836)` fails, it calls `tryListen(19837)`, which adds another listener. The listeners stack, but since each is `.once()`, only one fires. Safe, but slightly inefficient.

- **Server state ambiguity** (line 19): If `start()` is called twice, the early-exit prevents issues. But what if `start()` fails to bind any port? `this.server` remains set but never listens. The next `stop()` call won't error, but the API isn't actually running. Consider: after line 62, set `this.server = null` if all retries failed.

**Rating: SAFE** ✓

---

### 6. **src/main/mobile-server/websocket-manager.ts** — PTY Exit Broadcast & Cleanup

**(a) Does the fix look correct?** **YES**

**(b) Bugs introduced?** None critical.

The `broadcastPtyExit()` function (lines 318-342):
- Line 324: Checks if streams exist
- Lines 326-336: Sends exit message and closes each connection
- Line 340: Cleans up ptyStreams
- Line 341: Cleans up terminalSubscriptions (optional param)

This is correct cleanup.

**(c) Edge cases missed?**

- **Race between data and exit**: If `broadcastPtyData()` (line 284) is called while `broadcastPtyExit()` is executing, the streams map could change during iteration. However, JavaScript's `Map.forEach()` is safe against concurrent modifications — it doesn't break, but new entries added during iteration won't be iterated. Existing entries being deleted could cause issues, but in practice, this sequence is ordered by the PTY manager.

- **Double exit** (lines 340-341): If `broadcastPtyExit()` is called twice for the same ptyId, the second call will silently do nothing (the map entries are already deleted). Safe.

- **Missing error subscribers cleanup**: Line 341 cleans up `terminalSubscriptions` for a PTY, but there's also `ptyDataBuffer` (line 238 in `flushPtyBuffer`). The buffer is cleaned up when flushed, but if a PTY exits before a client connects, the buffer persists. Should add: `ptyDataBuffer.delete(ptyId)` in `broadcastPtyExit()`.

**Rating: SAFE** ✓ (minor: missing buffer cleanup)

---

### 7. **src/main/meta-project-sync.ts** — Stale Symlink Check

**(a) Does the fix look correct?** **YES**

**(b) Bugs introduced?** None detected.

The stale symlink detection:
- Lines 228-241: Iterate current symlinks, check if they match desired symlinks
- Line 233: `if (target === projectPath && name === desiredName)` — correct matching
- Lines 238-240: Remove symlinks that don't match

The symlink validation at lines 103-107 checks if the target path exists before creating.

**(c) Edge cases missed?**

- **Symlink circular references**: If a symlink target is itself a symlink chain that loops back, the creation might fail, but the check at line 104 only calls `existsSync()`, which follows symlinks. So circular symlinks would pass the check but might cause issues later. However, this is unlikely in practice.

- **Broken symlinks** (lines 229-241): The `readlinkSync()` at line 30 returns the target path even if it doesn't exist (broken symlink). The cleanup logic (lines 229-241) correctly removes them if they don't match desired targets. But what if a symlink target changed (e.g., `/project/a` now points to a different location)? The logic correctly detects this and removes the old symlink, then creates the new one. Good.

- **Permission errors** (line 34): If `lstatSync()` throws, the entry is skipped. Subsequent processing might try to remove it again. No issue, but logging the entry name would help debugging.

**Rating: SAFE** ✓

---

## Summary Table

| File | Issue | Severity | Status |
|------|-------|----------|--------|
| session-store.ts | Missing temp file cleanup on crash | Low | Document |
| workspace.ts | POST body wrapping mismatch with saveWorkspace | **CRITICAL** | **Fix immediately** |
| middleware.ts | Hostname case sensitivity (minor) | Very Low | Safe, consider lowercase() |
| kspec-adapter.ts | Global module state shared across instances | Moderate | **Refactor to instance state** |
| orchestrator-api.ts | Potential server state ambiguity if all ports fail | Low | Consider null-check after retries |
| websocket-manager.ts | Missing ptyDataBuffer cleanup in broadcastPtyExit | Low | Add buffer.delete() |
| meta-project-sync.ts | None | — | ✓ Safe |

**Critical Finding**: The workspace.ts POST handler has a field-wrapping bug that will create nested structures in the session store.
